Just now

Most businesses think a website is just an online brochure. In reality, your website is often the first salesperson customers meet. A poorly planned website can quietly cost leads, sales, and trust.
Here are the 10 most common mistakes businesses make when building their website.
1. Focusing on Design Instead of Conversions
A beautiful website means nothing if visitors don’t take action.
2. Slow Loading Speed
Even a one-second delay can increase bounce rates and reduce conversions.
3. No Clear Call-to-Action
Visitors should immediately know what to do next.
4. Ignoring Mobile Users
More than half of website traffic comes from mobile devices.
5. Weak SEO Foundation
Without SEO, even the best website can remain invisible.
6. Confusing Navigation
Users should find information within a few clicks.
7. Generic Content
Your content should address customer problems, not just talk about your company.
8. No Lead Capture System
Forms, booking systems, and contact options should be easy to find.
9. Poor Security Practices
An unsecured website damages trust.
10. Choosing the Wrong Technology Stack
Many businesses select platforms that limit future growth.
Final Thoughts
Your website should help your business generate leads, build trust, and support growth.
